articles.title: Intel shares fly on Apple chip deal report. Here's why it's a big deal
articles.description: Samsung, Intel and TSMC are the only three companies in the world capable of manufacturing the most advanced chips needed for AI.
articles.content:
Apple and Intel are reportedly closing in on a deal that would see Intel make some of the chips for the iPhone maker's devices, marking a major shift in the chipmaking landscape.

articles.title: N.J. man charged with creating AI-generated child sexual abuse material
articles.description: Authorities found more than 75 child sexual abuse material images on the West Deptford resident's personal cell phone, the Gloucester County Prosecutor’s Office said.
articles.content:
A man in Gloucester County was arrested and charged after police found dozens of child sexual abuse materials on his phone.

articles.title: 5,000 vibe-coded apps just proved shadow AI is the new S3 bucket crisis
articles.description: RedAccess found 380,000 exposed vibe-coded apps — roughly 5,000 contained sensitive corporate data including patient records and financial information.
articles.content: Most enterprise security programs were built to protect servers, endpoints, and cloud accounts. None of them was built to find a customer intake form that a product manager vibe coded on Lovable over a weekend, connected to a live Supabase database, ... [8916 chars]

articles.description: Platform engineering anchors Red Hat’s push to scale AI, virtualization and open hybrid cloud across enterprise infrastructure.

articles.title: India among first in Global South to develop Health AI benchmarking platform, says health body CEO
articles.description: India is among the first countries in the Global South to develop an open Health AI benchmarking platform to evaluate artificial intelligence models on diverse Indian datasets before deployment at a population scale, National Health Authority (NHA) CEO Dr Sunil Kumar Barnwal said on Friday.

articles.title: Star Wars' Mark Hamill branded 'sick' by White House after posting AI pic of Trump dead in a shallow grave
articles.description: STAR Wars legend Mark Hamill was branded a “sick individual” by The White House after posting an AI image of Donald Trump in a shallow...
articles.content:
STAR Wars legend Mark Hamill was branded a “sick individual” by The White House after posting an AI image of Donald Trump in a shallow grave.
The Luke Skywalker actor, 74 — an outspoken critic of the US President — later apologised and took it down.
